nuance_attributes.description: The level of familiarity or expertise of the reviewer with medical devices or sterilization procedures.
The environment or setting in which the reviewer used the device, such as clinical or training setting.
The tone and approach used in the review, ranging from technical and formal to casual and informal.
The amount of detail and specificity included in the review, from high-level summaries to in-depth analysis.
The intent behind the review, such as to inform, critique, or share user experience.
When the review is written relative to the device's use, such as immediately after use or after extended testing.
